evals.prompt: Write a Python function that fetches data from an API and handles errors
evals.expected_output: Typed Python function with proper error handling, type hints, and httpx or requests usage
expectations: Function has type hints for parameters and return type
Returns typed data (not raw dict unless necessary)

evals.prompt: Create a Python class that manages a collection of items with add, remove, and list operations
evals.expected_output: Clean class with type hints, immutability preference, and proper __init__
expectations: Prefers returning new collections over mutating in place
Does not use mutable default arguments

evals.prompt: Set up a new Python project with dependencies
evals.expected_output: Uses uv (not pip or poetry) for project setup
expectations: Does NOT use pip install or python -m venv

evals.prompt: Write a Go HTTP server that handles REST endpoints
evals.expected_output: Agent writes Go code, not Python — skill should not trigger
expectations: python-engineer skill does not interfere with Go tasks

evals.prompt: Write a Python dataclass for representing a user with name, email, and optional phone number
evals.expected_output: Dataclass with type hints, Optional for phone, and frozen=True preference
expectations: Has proper type hints (str, Optional[str] or str | None)
